role: This directory contains all source code for the ArchSpine mirror system, including infrastructure, core pipeline, engines, orchestration services, task stages, CLI interface, type definitions, utilities, AST analysis, and asset templates.
responsibility: Collectively, these components implement the full lifecycle of repository mirroring and architectural governance: from configuration and CLI interaction, through file scanning, AST extraction, dependency analysis, rule validation, LLM-based summarization and fix generation, to output generation of views and artifacts, while providing the foundational type contracts and utility support.

children.role: This directory represents the root of the ArchSpine mirror system, containing the agent skill definition for AI architectural awareness and the centralized template artifacts for governance, prompts, rules, and documentation.
AST analysis layer for the ArchSpine mirror system that provides language-specific parsing, symbol extraction, and language discovery capabilities.
This directory houses the command-line interface entrypoint, command routing, and utility modules for the ArchSpine system.
Core pipeline infrastructure and task execution engine for the ArchSpine system.
Core engine directory containing all primary processing, analysis, and reporting modules for the ArchSpine system.

Provides the foundational infrastructure layer for the ArchSpine mirror system, encompassing configuration, LLM interaction, database management, prompt building, security, file I/O, and integrity protection.
Orchestration services that coordinate the core pipelines (sync, check, fix) and manage runtime lifecycle, configuration, repository artifacts, and output generation for the ArchSpine mirror system.
This directory contains the individual stage task modules that together form the ArchSpine synchronization pipeline.
Core type definitions for the ArchSpine mirror system, covering protocol schemas and view output interfaces.

Core utilities and infrastructure modules for ArchSpine CLI, covering CLI presentation, filesystem operations, user prompts, git hook management, locking, path normalization, and agent instruction synchronization.
